QF.id: Securities Types Features and Asset Allocation Concepts_21

question.text: Investment in physical commodities like gold involves which of the following additional costs compared to equity and debt securities?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Unlike equity and debt securities, investment in physical forms of commodities involves storage and insurance costs. Such costs can be very high and make commodity investments out of reach of ordinary investors. Financialization of commodity investments (through ETFs, derivatives, SGBs) enables greater retail participation.
**The Trap:** While brokerage (option B) applies to buying/selling, it is not unique to commodities. Demat charges (option C) apply to securities, not physical commodities. SEBI does not charge annual fees to investors (option D).
**Cross-Exam Context:** Foundation topic tested across 22 NISM exams. Understanding commodity characteristics is relevant to NISM XII and the derivatives certification.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When clients want gold exposure, compare options: physical gold (storage/insurance costs), Gold ETFs (demat + AMC charges), SGBs (no storage cost + extra interest). Help them choose the most cost-efficient route.
